regions.us-ports: Today in Miami, easterly winds at 9 knots with partly cloudy skies and less than 10% rain probability will support normal embarkation and shore excursion operations throughout the day. Heat Advisory in effect for Manhattan and Brooklyn (heat index up to 104°F), Bayonne (heat index up to 101°F), and Boston (heat index up to 103°F); Extreme Heat Watch in effect for Philadelphia (heat index up to 108°F), Los Angeles and Long Beach (temperature up to 95°F), and San Diego (temperature up to 107°F). Over the next 48 to 72 hours, east-southeast winds will increase to 12 knots on Day 2 with rain showers and less than 10% rain probability, followed by easterly winds at 9 knots and overcast conditions on Day 3, with conditions remaining operationally favorable across the South Florida cruise corridor.
regions.bahamas-central-caribbean: Today heat index values are reaching 101 degrees across Nassau, Freeport, Bimini, and the Berry Islands, creating serious risk for passengers during outdoor shore excursions with ESE 8-knot winds and overcast skies despite less than 10% rain probability. Tomorrow heat index peaks at 100 degrees with E 8-knot winds and continued overcast conditions with less than 10% rain probability, affecting Key West, Grand Cayman, Ocho Rios, and Falmouth embarkation and water-based activities.
regions.eastern-caribbean: Today, San Juan, Puerto Rico reports E 12kt, overcast, less than 10% rain probability. A Heat Advisory is in effect for San Juan with heat index values reaching 104 degrees, posing a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions; today's easterly winds at 16 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability will allow port operations and embarkation to proceed normally, though the alert should prompt extended hydration breaks for excursion participants.
regions.western-caribbean: Today elevated heat index values reaching 101 degrees pose a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across the Western Caribbean, with ENE winds at 9 knots and 14% rain probability allowing generally favorable conditions for port operations in Cozumel, Costa Maya, Roatan, and Belize City. Tomorrow E winds at 9 knots with 29% rain probability may affect water-based activities, though drizzle conditions should remain manageable for most embarkation and disembarkation operations. By day three, ENE winds increase to 11 knots with rain showers and 40% rain probability expected to impact outdoor excursions across all ports, requiring contingency planning for covered or rescheduled activities.
regions.southern-caribbean: Today easterly winds at 18 knots with overcast skies and 28% rain probability will allow standard port operations and shore excursions to proceed in Aruba, though passengers should anticipate occasional light drizzle. Day 2 brings elevated easterly winds reaching 21 knots across the Southern Caribbean with 11% rain probability, affecting tender operations and water-based excursions particularly in Aruba and Curacao. Day 3 escalates to 22-knot easterly winds with thunderstorms developing and 16% rain probability, creating rougher sea conditions that may delay embarkation and disembarkation procedures at all four ports.
regions.lesser-antilles: Today easterly winds at 13 knots will support normal port operations and tender services across Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada, with overcast skies and 21% rain probability posing no operational constraint. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, conditions improve significantly as ENE winds ease to 12 knots on Day 2 with less than 10% rain probability, favoring all shore excursion activity throughout the region. Beyond 48 hours, easterly winds return to 13 knots with 11% rain probability on Day 3, maintaining settled conditions for continued port calls and water-based activities across all Lesser Antilles anchorages.
regions.los-angeles: Today westerly winds will increase to 12 knots with overcast skies developing across Los Angeles, Long Beach, Marina del Rey, and Catalina Island, creating manageable conditions for embarkation and tender operations with less than 10% rain probability. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, westerly winds will maintain 12 knots while skies clear at all four ports, supporting normal shore excursion schedules with less than 10% rain probability persisting throughout. Beyond 48 hours, southwesterly winds will moderate to 9 knots and partly cloudy skies will prevail, sustaining favorable conditions for cruise operations with less than 10% rain probability continuing into Day 3.
regions.ensenada: Today light westerly winds at 8 knots will develop under overcast skies with less than 10% rain probability, allowing port operations and tender services at Ensenada, Punta Banda, and Islas Todos Santos to proceed normally with minimal weather delay. Over the next 24-48 hours, westerly winds remain steady at 8 knots while fog develops on Day 2, which may reduce visibility for inbound and outbound vessel transits but will not restrict port access or shore excursion scheduling. Beyond 48 hours, westerly flow persists at 8 knots with improving sky conditions to partly cloudy on Day 3 and less than 10% rain probability maintained throughout the forecast window.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Today elevated heat with index values reaching 104 degrees poses serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across Cabo San Lucas, San Jose del Cabo, and La Paz, with southwest winds at 7 knots and partly cloudy skies supporting port operations and embarkation activities. Day 2 continues the heat threat with index values reaching 102 degrees under southwest 8-knot winds and partly cloudy conditions, maintaining favorable conditions for tender operations at all three ports. By Day 3, westerly winds increase to 11 knots with drizzle developing and 19% rain probability, which may affect afternoon excursions, though overall port accessibility remains uncompromised.
regions.mazatlan: Today, southerly winds at 8 kt will accompany thunderstorms with 43% rain probability, potentially affecting shore excursions and tender operations at Mazatlan, Topolobampo, and Altata. Over the next 24-48 hours, northwesterly winds shift to 7 kt while a 64% rain probability is expected to impact port operations and passenger movement across all three ports on day two. Beyond 48 hours, northwesterly winds ease to 6 kt with thunderstorms persisting at 36% rain probability, gradually moderating conditions for cruise operations by day three.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Today thunderstorms with 49% rain probability may affect port operations and shore excursions across Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala, with ENE winds at 7 kt maintaining light conditions for embarkation and disembarkation. Over the next 48 hours, N winds at 8 kt on Day 2 will accompany thunderstorms with 39% rain probability, potentially impacting water-based activities, while Day 3 transitions to E winds at 7 kt with thunderstorms and 34% rain probability as activity frequency decreases. Beyond 48 hours, the easterly wind pattern persists with declining thunderstorm coverage, though isolated convection remains possible through the extended outlook period.
regions.western-mediterranean: Today easterly winds at 8 knots and overcast skies with less than 10% rain probability support normal port operations and shore excursions across Barcelona, Valencia, Palma de Mallorca, Ibiza, Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar. Over the next 24-48 hours, light southeasterly winds of 9 knots will develop with foggy conditions and less than 10% rain probability, potentially reducing visibility for embarkation and disembarkation at all ports but maintaining safe transit conditions. Beyond 48 hours, southwesterly winds at 8 knots with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability indicate a continuation of stable, passenger-friendly conditions throughout the region.
regions.central-mediterranean: Today, elevated heat with heat index reaching 100F poses a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions across the central Mediterranean, with northerly winds at 11 knots and partly cloudy skies limiting relief at ports including Naples, Civitavecchia, and Sardinia. Over the next 24-48 hours, southwesterly winds shift to 8 knots with continued partly cloudy conditions and less than 10% rain probability, maintaining heat index values near 100F and affecting embarkation and disembarkation operations throughout the region including Venice, Dubrovnik, and Split.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Today heat index values are reaching 100 degrees across the Eastern Mediterranean, posing a serious risk to passengers during outdoor shore excursions in Athens, Santorini, Mykonos, Rhodes, and other ports, with ENE winds at 8 knots and overcast skies limiting relief. Through day two, NNE winds increase to 10 knots with partly cloudy conditions and less than 10% rain probability maintaining stable port operations and embarkation schedules throughout the region. By day three, heat index again reaches 100 degrees with NNE winds diminishing to 5 knots and partly cloudy skies continuing, requiring continued passenger heat precautions but sustaining favorable conditions for all cruise operations.
regions.southeast-alaska: Today light winds from the west-southwest at 4 knots with overcast skies and 23% rain probability will support normal port operations and shore excursion activities in Juneau, Ketchikan, and Skagway. Over the next 24 to 48 hours, winds will increase slightly to 6 knots from the west-northwest with less than 10% rain probability, maintaining favorable conditions for cruise operations throughout the region. By day three, northeast winds at 3 knots will accompany drizzle with 36% rain probability, which may affect outdoor activities in all ports.
